title: juncus lampocarpus subsp. macrocephalus
intro: Juncus lampocarpus subsp. macrocephalus, commonly known as giant club rush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Juncaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of giant club rush can be made into infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and can be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and gastrointestinal issues.
